steps.title: Identify the base growth factor per 3-unit increase
steps.content: The function is $$f(x) = 26 \cdot (1.20)^{x/3}$$. Increasing $x$ by 3 adds 1 to the exponent, so the function is multiplied by $1.20$: $$f(x+3) = 26 \cdot (1.20)^{(x+3)/3} = 26 \cdot (1.20)^{x/3} \cdot 1.20 = 1.20 \cdot f(x)$$.

steps.title: Compute the factor for a 6-unit increase and convert to percent increase
steps.content: Increasing $x$ by 6 adds $6/3 = 2$ to the exponent, so the factor is $(1.20)^2$. $$(1.20)^2 = 1.44$$. The percent increase $p$ satisfies $1 + p/100 = 1.44$, giving $$p = (1.44 - 1) \times 100 = 44$$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
